On page 4, in the '3.1. Demographics and disease characteristics at baseline' section, it further confirms: 'A total of 1052 patients were randomised in a 1:1 ratio to receive either 240 mg/day apalutamide (n = 525) or placebo (n = 527), plus ongoing ADT...' Table 1 on page 4 also lists 'Placebo + ADT (n = 527)' as the control arm.

The 'Introduction' on page 2, lines 58-59 mentions 'androgen receptor (AR) pathway inhibitor' in relation to treatment. Therefore, Treatment Arm 1 consists of Apalutamide (an AR pathway inhibitor) and Androgen Deprivation Therapy (ADT).

Total Participants - N.evidence: On page 4, under section '3.1. Demographics and disease characteristics at baseline', it states: 'A total of 1052 patients were randomised in a 1:1 ratio to receive either 240 mg/day apalutamide (n = 525) or placebo (n = 527), plus ongoing ADT...' This indicates the total number of participants.

Treatment Arm - N.evidence: On page 2, in the 'Methods' section, it states: '...patients receiving 240 mg/day apalutamide (n = 525) or placebo (n = 527) plus androgen-deprivation therapy (ADT).' This indicates 525 participants in the apalutamide (experimental) arm. This is further confirmed on page 4, in section '3.1. Demographics and disease characteristics at baseline', which states: 'A total of 1052 patients were randomised in a 1:1 ratio to receive either 240 mg/day apalutamide (n = 525) or placebo (n = 527), plus ongoing ADT...'.

Control Arm.evidence: On page 2, in the 'Methods' section, it states: 'These protocol-defined and post hoc analyses of the phase III randomised TITAN study evaluated clinical outcomes in patients receiving 240 mg/day apalutamide (n = 525) or placebo (n = 527) plus androgen-deprivation therapy (ADT).' This indicates that the control arm, which did not receive the experimental treatment (apalutamide), was 'placebo plus androgen-deprivation therapy (ADT)'. This is further supported by Table 1 on page 4, which lists 'Placebo + ADT' as one of the treatment groups.

Number of Arms Included.evidence: The 'Methods' section on page 2 states that the study evaluated clinical outcomes in patients receiving '240 mg/day apalutamide (n = 525) or placebo (n = 527) plus androgen-deprivation therapy (ADT)', indicating two distinct treatment arms.

This indicates that the experimental arm received apalutamide plus ADT. This is further supported by the 'Results' section on page 4, lines 14-16, which mentions patients were randomised 'to receive either 240 mg/day apalutamide (n = 525) or placebo (n = 527), plus ongoing ADT'.

NCT.evidence: Found on page 2, line 25: "Clinical Trial Registration: NCT02489318." and on page 3, line 4: "In TITAN (ClinicalTrials.com: NCT02489318)".

PubMed ID.evidence: PubMed ID is not explicitly reported in the document. A DOI is provided, but not a PubMed ID.

Trial Name.evidence: The main title on page 1, lines 11-16, refers to "A subgroup analysis of the randomised clinical TITAN study". The study is consistently referred to as TITAN throughout the document, for example, on page 2, line 24 and page 3, line 4.

Author.evidence: The authors are listed on page 1, lines 20-26. Axel S. Merseburger is the first author, followed by a long list of co-authors, making 'et al.' appropriate.

Year.evidence: Found on page 1, line 43: "Received 30 March 2023; Received in revised form 31 July 2023; Accepted 6 August 2023" and on page 1, line 56: "© 2023 The Authors. Published by Elsevier Ltd."

Full Pub or Abstract.evidence: The document is labeled "ARTICLE IN PRESS" on page 1 and page 2, and contains a comprehensive structure including abstract, methods, results, discussion, and references, indicating a full publication.

Phase.evidence: Found on page 3, line 5: "In TITAN (ClinicalTrials.com: NCT02489318), a phase III, randomised, double-blind placebo-controlled study..." and on page 2, line 12: "...analyses of the phase III randomised TITAN study..."

Original/Follow Up.evidence: The title on page 1, lines 11-16, explicitly states: "A subgroup analysis of the randomised clinical TITAN study", indicating it is a follow-up analysis of an original trial. This is further supported by page 3, lines 17-18: "Herein, we report an updated and detailed assessment...within specific TITAN subgroups..."

Quality of Life Scale.evidence: The document focuses on clinical outcomes such as overall survival (OS), radiographic progression-free survival (rPFS), time to PSA progression, and safety (treatment-emergent adverse events). While 'Mean baseline BPI-SF pain score' is mentioned in Table 1 on page 4, this is a specific pain assessment and not a general 'Quality of Life Scale' reflecting overall well-being and satisfaction with health status as defined in the prompt. No other standardized questionnaire or survey for overall quality of life is reported.

Trial.evidence: The title of the article on page 1, lines 14-17, mentions 'A subgroup analysis of the randomised clinical TITAN study'. The abstract on page 2, lines 13-15, refers to 'TITAN patients'. The 'Material and methods' section on page 3, lines 4-6, states 'In TITAN (ClinicalTrials.com: NCT02489318), a phase III, randomised, double-blind placebo-controlled study...' This consistently identifies 'TITAN' as the name of the clinical study.

Quality of Life reported.evidence: The document describes various clinical outcomes such as overall survival (OS), radiographic or second progression-free survival (rPFS, PFS2), time to prostate-specific antigen (PSA) progression, castration resistance, and treatment-emergent adverse events (TEAEs). While Table 1 on page 4 includes 'Mean baseline BPI-SF pain score' (Brief Pain Inventory - Short Form) as a baseline characteristic, it does not report results or outcomes related to the quality of life of participants as a measured endpoint of the trial.

Median Age (years) | Treatment.evidence: On page 4, Table 1, under 'Baseline demographics and disease characteristics', the row 'Median age in years (range)' shows '69 (45-94)' for 'Apalutamide + ADT (n = 525)' (Treatment arm). The median age is 69 years.

Median Age (years) | Control.evidence: On page 4, Table 1, under 'Baseline demographics and disease characteristics', the row 'Median age in years (range)' shows '68 (43-90)' for 'Placebo + ADT (n = 527)' (Control arm). The median age is 68 years.

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | S.evidence: The 'Methods' section on page 2 states: 'Subgroups were defined by... timing of metastasis presentation (synchronous: at initial diagnosis; metachronous: after localised disease)'. The 'Results' section on page 2 reports OS benefit for 'synchronous/high-volume' and 'synchronous/low-volume' subgroups. Table 1 on page 4 also lists 'Synchronous', 'Synchronous/high-volume', and 'Synchronous/low-volume' under 'Metastatic presentation at initial diagnosis'. Figure 1 on page 5 presents Kaplan-Meier plots for 'Synchronous/high-volume' and 'Synchronous/low-volume' for OS and rPFS.

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | M.evidence: The 'Methods' section on page 2 states: 'Subgroups were defined by... timing of metastasis presentation (synchronous: at initial diagnosis; metachronous: after localised disease)'. The 'Results' section on page 2 reports OS benefit for 'metachronous/high-volume' and 'metachronous/low-volume' subgroups. Table 1 on page 4 also lists 'Metachronous', 'Metachronous/high-volume', and 'Metachronous/low-volume' under 'Metastatic presentation at initial diagnosis'. Figure 1 on page 5 presents Kaplan-Meier plots for 'Metachronous/high-volume' and 'Metachronous/low-volume' for OS and rPFS.

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | H.evidence: The 'Methods' section on page 2 states: 'Subgroups were defined by volume (high: visceral and ≥1 bone metastases or ≥4 bone lesions with ≥1 beyond vertebral column/pelvis)'. The 'Results' section on page 2 reports that '63%... had high-volume... disease' and presents OS benefit for 'synchronous/high-volume' and 'metachronous/high-volume' subgroups. Table 1 on page 4 lists 'High' under 'Disease volume' and also 'Synchronous/high-volume' and 'Metachronous/high-volume'. Figure 1 on page 5 presents Kaplan-Meier plots for 'Synchronous/high-volume' and 'Metachronous/high-volume' for OS and rPFS.

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | L.evidence: The 'Methods' section on page 2 states: 'Patients who did not meet the high-volume disease criteria were considered to have low-volume disease'. The 'Results' section on page 2 presents OS benefit for 'synchronous/low-volume' and 'metachronous/low-volume' subgroups. Table 1 on page 4 lists 'Low' under 'Disease volume' and also 'Synchronous/low-volume' and 'Metachronous/low-volume'. Figure 1 on page 5 presents Kaplan-Meier plots for 'Synchronous/low-volume' and 'Metachronous/low-volume' for OS and rPFS.

Primary Endpoint(s).evidence: On page 3, in the '2. Material and methods' section, lines 37-38 state: 'Dual primary end-points were rPFS and OS'.

Secondary Endpoint(s).value: Exploratory end-points included time to PSA progression and PFS2. Other assessed outcomes included confirmed PSA decline (decline of ≥50% or ≥90% from baseline or decline to ≤0.2 ng/mL achieved at any time during the study and confirmed ≥4 weeks later) and time to castration resistance.
Secondary Endpoint(s).evidence: On page 3, section '2. Material and methods', lines 37-40 state: 'Dual primary end-points were rPFS and OS; exploratory end-points included time to PSA progression and PFS2.' Lines 45-53 further detail other assessed outcomes: 'confirmed PSA decline (decline of ≥50% or ≥90% from baseline or decline to ≤0.2 ng/mL achieved at any time during the study and confirmed ≥4 weeks later), time to PSA progression (time from date of randomisation to date of PSA progression based on Prostate Cancer Working Group 2 (PCWG2) criteria [27]) and time to castration resistance (time from date of randomisation to date of PSA progression, based on PCWG2 criteria [27]).'

There is no mention of an 'overall risk judgment of individual criteria of evidence (COE)' in randomized controlled trials (RCTs) as a general concept or a specific assessment within this document.

Type of Therapy.evidence: The title of the article on page 1, lines 12-13, states 'Apalutamide plus androgen deprivation therapy'. The abstract's methods section on page 2, lines 10-14, further specifies 'patients receiving 240 mg/day apalutamide (n = 525) or placebo (n = 527) plus androgen-deprivation therapy (ADT)'.

Add-on Treatment.evidence: The title of the article is "Apalutamide plus androgen deprivation therapy in clinical subgroups of patients with metastatic castration-sensitive prostate cancer". The abstract states in the Methods section: "...patients receiving 240 mg/day apalutamide (n = 525) or placebo (n = 527) plus androgen-deprivation therapy (ADT)." This indicates that Apalutamide is the additional therapy given in combination with the primary treatment (ADT).
